message:
fix(ui): gate rich-ui imports and rendering behind unix cfg

The rich_rust crate uses Unix-specific terminal APIs (ioctl, termios)
that fail to compile on Windows. Previous commit 621d0bd gated the UI
modules themselves, but missed the conditional compilation guards on
the import statements and inline render calls in three files:

- rch/src/hook.rs: Panel import and job banner rich rendering
  (2 sites: use statement + emit_job_banner body)
- rch/src/ui/benchmark.rs: prelude/Table/Column/Row imports and all
  BenchmarkTable rich rendering methods (7 sites total)
- rch/src/ui/config.rs: prelude import and ConfigDisplay rich rendering
  methods (4 sites total)

All #[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")] guards are now
#[cfg(all(feature = "rich-ui", unix))] to match the pattern established
in the prior fix. This ensures the Windows CI matrix passes without
needing to disable the rich-ui feature entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
